design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team, play fast-paced ice hockey matches with arcade-style controls, engage in fighting minigame when collisions trigger combat, advance through tournament brackets to win championship cup
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Physical collision and fighting system creates emergent confrontation—bump opponents three times without hitting others to initiate pre-fight exchanges that can escalate into full combat, creating risk/reward for aggressive play
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Fast-paced arcade hockey action with emphasis on physicality and fighting; dominate opponents through both skilled play and combat prowess
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately prioritizes fighting and physical contact over simulation—no offside rule, abstract penalty system, fighting generates power-play advantages (5-on-4 to 5-on-1). Game mechanics explicitly celebrate confrontation as core gameplay rather than aberration. Treats fighting as strategic tool, not simulation deviation.

difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament mode progresses through playoff-style bracket; matches are discrete units
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty levels available at game start: Junior (easiest), College, Pro (hardest) when playing against computer opponent

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament mode requires sequential wins against progressively challenging teams in playoff bracket format to win championship cup; Exhibition mode allows single matches
replayability_hooks: Eight fictional teams based on real NHL cities (Toronto, Vancouver, Montreal, Edmonton, New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, Minnesota) with distinct jersey colors
Two-player competitive mode for head-to-head play
Three difficulty tiers for computer opponent
Penalty shootout tiebreaker system requires skill execution

world_building_techniques: Fictional team names anchored to real North American cities and NHL historical context (late 1980s)
Championship cup award framing tournament progression with aspirational sports fantasy

faction_indicators: Eight distinct teams with city identities (Canadian vs American split: 4 each)
Team selection at game start signals player affiliation
danger_signals: Fighting system creates physical danger states—opponent knockout triggers penalty box for loser
Power-play advantages (5-on-4 to 5-on-1) emerge from fight outcomes
Pre-fight confrontation sequence with punch exchange determines if full fight initiates
environmental_storytelling: Jersey color variation between teams establishes visual identity hierarchy
Penalty box relegation provides spatial punishment for fight losers
Rink setting (mentioned in FDS title screen's overhead animated view) grounds action in hockey arena context

sources_used: tcrf - Blades of Steel regional differences documentation
